response: convert The original tax for the art collection is calculated by applying the 5% discount to the 20% rate, resulting in a 19% effective rate on $2 million, which equals $380,000. convert The real estate portfolio originally valued at $5 million incurs a tax of $100,000 on the first million, $300,000 on the next two million, and $400,000 on the remaining two million, totaling $800,000. convert The securities portfolio tax is determined by subtracting the $100,000 deduction from the $3 million value to get a taxable base of $2.9 million, then applying the 15% rate for a liability of $435,000. convert Summing these components yields an initial total tax liability of $1,615,000 for the estate before corrections. convert The misvaluation error requires increasing the real estate value by 10% to $5.5 million, which shifts $500,000 of the asset into the highest 20% tax bracket. convert This adjustment adds an additional $100,000 in tax on the real estate portion, bringing the new real estate liability to $900,000. convert Adding the unchanged art and securities taxes to the corrected real estate figure results in a final total tax liability of $1,715,000.
